completion_criteria_checked: task-state.json (and schema) gain a structured way to record an owner-decision request: at minimum a needed/not-needed flag, the decision question, the reason it's needed, the options being weighed, and what stays blocked until the owner decides.
The structure is optional/nullable when no owner decision is pending, so ordinary tasks are unaffected.

The generated worker directive and advisor restart brief surface an active owner-decision request clearly when one is present, so a fresh worker or advisor session sees it without re-deriving it from prose.

The feature is demonstrated: an owner-decision request is populated for a real or realistic scenario and shown rendering correctly in both artifacts, then cleared/demonstrated absent for the normal case.
Truth-surface propagation is handled honestly across docs/DECISIONS.md, docs/BRR_PLAN.md, docs/BRR_POLICY.md, docs/STATE_MODEL.md, docs/HANDOFF_PACKET_SPEC.md, docs/REPO_GOVERNANCE.md, and README.md.
No automatic stop-trigger detection, owner-decision routing/notification, or acceptance-boundary enforcement is introduced.
Local validation remains healthy on the actual returned-for-verification state.
risks: Self-verified under DECISION-033 degraded fallback (Codex unavailable). No independent second-party (Codex) review occurred. GPT secondary review: not performed this cycle (not yet pushed at time of verification).

The demonstration used a disposable scratch copy with a synthetic (though genuinely open) owner-decision example, not a live-populated one, since this task's own drafting had no ambiguity requiring a real owner-decision request. This is disclosed directly in the worker evidence rather than hidden.
owner_decision_request and current_blocker can be populated independently with no cross-validation between them; a future cycle could set them inconsistently without any check catching it.
summary: Independently re-ran scripts/verify-handback-guardrails.ps1 against the actual returned-for-verification state (clean; one expected, non-fatal WARN about the handoff gate referencing the prior task). Verified via git status that only the claimed files changed - no BRR_PLAN.md edit (correctly, matching established precedent that it carries no per-item completion annotations), no unrelated scripts or schemas touched. Independently reviewed the schema addition, both generator changes, and all five touched docs, confirming the owner_decision_request field's null/populated behavior, its distinction from current_blocker, and consistent propagation across all touched truth surfaces. All completion criteria met.
next_action: Task 'pcc-brr2-007' is complete and verified PASS. Close out per docs/HANDOFF_PACKET_SPEC.md's Recommended Close-Out Order, then commit and push per DECISION-036's time-boxed authorization.
